message:
test(fcp-core): pin stable identifiers, health-state ladder, and bootstrap-error variant count

Adds three new pinning tests covering surface-stable types whose Display /
roundtrip / aggregation contract is part of the public protocol, and
tightens an existing pinning test to fail when new error variants are added
without coverage.

New tests:

- crates/fcp-core/tests/credential_id_display.rs
    - credential_id_display_parse_roundtrips: round-trips a canonical UUID
      through CredentialId::parse / from_uuid / Display, ensuring the wire
      form survives parse → format → re-parse.
    - credential_id_parse_canonicalizes_uuid_case: confirms uppercase and
      mixed-case UUID inputs canonicalize to the same lowercase Display
      output and compare equal to a canonical CredentialId.
    Covers the contract that two structurally identical credential IDs
    serialize identically on the wire regardless of how they were
    constructed.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/principal_id_roundtrip.rs
    - principal_ids_with_same_kind_and_literal_compare_equal: asserts
      PrincipalId::new, FromStr, and TryFrom<String> all produce the same
      value for a given "kind:literal" string and round-trip through
      as_str().
    - principal_ids_with_different_kinds_do_not_compare_equal: locks in that
      "user:alice", "agent:alice", and "service:alice" are three distinct
      principals — the kind prefix participates in equality.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/health_state_transitions.rs
    - aggregate_health_state_degrades_through_documented_ladder: walks a
      single-component CompositeHealthSnapshot through the documented ladder
      Healthy → Degraded → Unhealthy and asserts the aggregate reports the
      same state, pinning the aggregation rule that "the worst component
      drives the aggregate" for the trivial 1-component case.

Tightening:

- crates/fcp-core/tests/bootstrap_error_display.rs grows a
  BOOTSTRAP_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT = 27 constant; the display-pinning test now
  asserts cases.len() == BOOTSTRAP_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT before iterating.
  Adding a new BootstrapError variant without extending display_cases() will
  now fail this test loudly instead of silently leaving the new variant
  unpinned.

No production code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
